trivia_bullets.text: Furor of the Bitten originated in Innistrad (September 2011) — the gothic horror plane of Innistrad, home to the game's most developed werewolf mechanics. The original printing (ISD 143) was part of the transform-era werewolf design space, where red enchantments fueled the berserker-aggro side of the human/lycanthrope identity. The M21 reprint carries the same Randy Gallegos art and flavor text. [Scryfall: set=isd, released_at=2011-09-30]

Only two printings exist: Innistrad (2011) and Core Set 2021 (2020), both illustrated by Randy Gallegos. The card was reprinted into M21 with no art or text changes, a nine-year gap between printings. [Scryfall: prints_search_uri]

"Those who only believe themselves infected with lycanthropy can be as dangerous as those who really are." [Scryfall] No attributed speaker. The flavor text exploits the ambiguity of psychosomatic transformation — the enchantment's mechanical effect (forced attacks, +2/+2) literalizes the loss of agency regardless of whether the curse is 'real,' which makes the card thematically coherent as both a horror and a red enchantment.

EDHREC rank 18,512 [Scryfall: edhrec_rank] — the lowest rank in this five-card batch, indicating it sees less EDH play than the other M21 commons here. Pauper-legal at {R} with +2/+2 and forced attacks; the forced-attack drawback is the main limiting factor in competitive Pauper, where it can give opponents too much control information.
